title: The Ember Road
premise: Waystone glyphs from an old Cinder-Seeker relay still mark a route through the land. The road-keeper who carved them hid tools at each stage. The final cache was never claimed.

steps.journalHint: The inscription described a fire bracket on a scorched mountain ridge — the road-keeper's first signal point. Find the ridge where iron was once bolted to stone.
Above the signal ridge, the keeper's next mark was a stone shelter against the snowline — a halfway house where signal cord was stored for the road.
The keeper's log route led through the ruins to a wayhouse — the place where the last signal fire burned before the Searing crossed. Find the sealed alcove in the rubble.
The road's last waystone marks a stone hollow in the ruins — the final cache point. The keeper's note said 'Come quickly' and sealed something there when the Searing came.

title: The Salt Covenant
premise: Two settlements once signed a survival compact on a salt shelf in the wastes. The original record — and the cache it sealed — was never recovered. Someone scratched out one of the names.

steps.journalHint: The map's first mark is a salt shelf in the wastes — wide and flat, the kind of ground people once gathered to trade on. Look for a boundary stone at the shelf's center.
The boundary stone named a second compact site — a watchtower near the wastes edge. The scratched-out name pointed toward it. Find the tower and what was hidden under the floor.
One of the letters named a third compact witness who sheltered in a sealed storeroom in the ruins south of the tower. Find the storeroom and what they left behind.
The storeroom walls show a hand-drawn border line ending at an old road crossing in the ruins. The covenant was signed there — look for a marked flagstone.
The covenant described a sealed cache under the first arch of the southern ruins — the actual goods traded as surety, hidden there and never collected. Find the arch.

title: Dead Reckoning
premise: A cartographer's final notebook describes three waypoints still accessible. They left their pack sealed at the last crossing and never came back for it.

steps.journalHint: The cartographer's first waypoint was a three-stone cairn at a river bend in the forest. Look for it where water curves against old ground.
The circled mark on the partial map leads to a valley the cartographer noted as 'echo walls — sound carries far.' Find the valley where stone walls return your voice.
A note in the second tube mentioned 'the crossing' — where the old road meets the river. Half the pack was left there under a compass-rose flagstone.
The final waypoint is a cliff above the ruins — the cartographer's last sight line. The note said the view from the top shows the way out.

title: The Warden's Seal
premise: Before the Searing crossed the first settlements, a warden attempted a sealing ritual at the border. The ritual failed and the warden vanished. The mail they wore was never found.

steps.journalHint: The frieze showed the warden at a gate — one of the old border crossings in the settlement ruins. Find a gate with warden's marks on the lintel.
The order named the wayhouse — a sheltered building near the old road, east of the gate. The warden was last seen heading there.
The log's last entry mentioned a 'hollow place' near the ruins where old rites were held. The warden brought the mail there. Find the hollow.
The warden's note said to look above the first arch in the ruins when the light holds. Climb to where daylight reaches the stone corbels.

title: The Fire Keeper's Cord
premise: Before the Searing swallowed the last shrine, the fire-keeper knotted their prayers into a cord and hid it along a route only they knew. The cord still holds. So do the prayers.

steps.journalHint: The shrine thread ended at a 'low brook altar' — a carved stump beside running water deep in the forest. Follow running water until you find the altar.
The keeper's route continued into overgrown old growth — a natural hollow among ancient trees where something was sealed in a tin. Find the hollow at the heart of the oldest grove.
The note directed east to 'the place of old ash' — a settlement ruin where the keeper lit the festival fire each year. Find the hearth ring in the ruins.
The keeper's route crossed a stretch of scarred wastes on the way out of the ruins — a road where fire was once lit for departing travelers. Find the route marker they left there.
The last counting loop pointed toward a 'cold hollow' the keeper called 'the only ground that did not shake.' Head into the hills above the wastes.
